title: rubus milliformis subsp. cyclophyllus
intro: Rubus milliformis subsp. cyclophyllus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry bush, is a subspecies of the Rubus milliformis plant, which is a type of bramble native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rubus milliformis subsp. cyclophyllus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for overall well-being. Rubus milliformis subsp. cyclophyllus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and prevent illness.
